title: GI Bill vs VR&E Strategy Comparator
desc: You are deciding whether to use Chapter 33 first, pursue VR&E first, or preserve benefits for later schooling and support needs.
problemRouterLabel: Should I use GI Bill or VR&E first?

steps.title: Should I use GI Bill or VR&E first?

fields.name: vreStatus
fields.label: Are you already entitled to VR&E or only eligible to apply?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Eligible to apply, not yet found entitled

fields.name: chapter33Remaining
fields.label: How many months of Post-9/11 GI Bill remain?

fields.name: supportNeeds
fields.label: Do you need tuition only, or also equipment, accommodations, counseling, and job-placement support?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Need laptop, counseling, and school coordination

fields.name: futureSchooling
fields.label: Do you expect future schooling after this program?
fields.placeholder: e.g. May need graduate school later

fields.name: preserveReason
fields.label: Are you trying to preserve Chapter 33 months or avoid retroactive induction issues later?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Want to preserve Ch33 for doctorate

intakeQuestions: Are you already entitled to VR&E or only eligible to apply?
How many months of Post-9/11 GI Bill remain?
Do you need tuition, books, equipment, accommodations, or counseling support?
Do you expect future schooling after this program?
Are you trying to preserve GI Bill months?
Would using GI Bill first create a retroactive induction issue later?
likelyVaErrors: The strategy decision may be getting reduced to monthly housing amount alone instead of overall rehabilitation support and future benefit preservation.
The veteran may not have compared restoration or retroactive-induction implications before choosing benefit order.
generatedTools: Question list for initial VRC appointment
recordsToRequest: Program cost, equipment, and accommodation needs list
Long-term schooling plan if additional education may be needed later
errors: Veterans often compare only tuition or housing rates instead of the full rehabilitation support package.
Choosing the wrong benefit order early can reduce flexibility for future training or retroactive restoration arguments.

evidenceChecklist.text: List of non-tuition supports that Chapter 31 may be better positioned to provide
Future education plan showing why preserving Chapter 33 may matter
Evidence or reasoning showing whether retroactive induction could matter later
